summary: Completed frontend testing for mobile audio button bug fix on Landing.js. The fix removed undefined variables (audioReady, audioProgress) and simplified the button to use only audioPlaying and audioLoading states. All tests passed: button visible and clickable on desktop and mobile, correct state transitions (green→loading→yellow→green), audio endpoints return HTTP 200, French/English language switching works correctly.
passed_tests: Desktop: Voiceover button (data-testid='voiceover-btn') is visible and clickable
Desktop: Button shows correct initial state (green background, 'Listen' text)
Desktop: Button transitions to playing state (yellow background, 'Stop' text) on click
Desktop: Button returns to initial state when clicked again to stop
Mobile (390x844): Voiceover button is visible and clickable
Mobile: Button shows correct initial state (green background)
Mobile: Button transitions to playing state (yellow background) on click
Mobile: Button returns to initial state when stopped
French language: Button shows 'Écouter' text with French flag
French language: Button state transitions work correctly
No JavaScript errors found on page
critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix verified: Removed undefined variables audioReady and audioProgress from JSX
Button now correctly uses only audioPlaying and audioLoading states declared via useState
playVoiceover() function (lines 46-93) properly handles audio loading, playing, and stopping
Audio files exist in /app/frontend/public/audio/voiceover/ for all supported languages
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile audio button bug fix verified. The voiceover button in Landing.js video section now works correctly on both desktop and mobile. Button states: initial (green, 'Écouter'/'Listen'), loading (gray, 'Chargement...'/'Loading...'), playing (yellow #FFD60A, 'Stop'). Audio files served from /audio/voiceover/voiceover_{lang}.mp3.

bug_fix_verification.bug_description: Mobile audio button was frozen/blocked due to undefined variables audioReady and audioProgress causing JavaScript error
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Simplified button JSX to use only audioPlaying and audioLoading states which are properly declared via useState
bug_fix_verification.verification: Button now works correctly on both desktop and mobile with proper state transitions
